# Social Links

The Social Links component displays a horizontal list of social media icons that link to various social media platforms. Each icon is properly labeled for accessibility and provides a clear, recognizable visual link to the corresponding platform.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the Social Links properties in real-time.

<Canvas of={SocialLinksStories.SocialLinks} />
<Controls of={SocialLinksStories.SocialLinks} />

## Properties

### Required Properties

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Property</th>
      <th>Type</th>
      <th>Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>
        <code>icons</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>array</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        Array of social media link objects. Each link should contain{' '}
        <code>url</code>, <code>name</code>, and <code>icon</code>
      </td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

### Individual Icon Properties

Each object in the `icons` array should contain:

<table>
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>Property</th>
      <th>Type</th>
      <th>Description</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <td>
        <code>url</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>string</code>
      </td>
      <td>The URL destination for the social media platform</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        <code>name</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>string</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        Visually hidden text for screen readers that identifies the social media
        platform
      </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        <code>icon</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        <code>string</code>
      </td>
      <td>
        The icon identifier for the social media platform (e.g., 'facebook',
        'x-twitter', 'linkedin')
      </td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

## Supported Platforms

The Social Links component supports icons for the following social media platforms:

- X (formerly Twitter)
- Facebook
- YouTube
- Instagram
- Weibo
- LinkedIn
- Bluesky

Additional platforms can be supported by extending the icon system.

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Molecule
- **Template**: `yds-social-links.twig`
- **Styles**: `_yds-social-links.scss`

### Accessibility

- Semantic list structure (`<ul>` and `<li>` elements) for proper screen reader navigation
- Visually hidden text provides context for each social media link
- Icons are implemented as background images with proper labels
- Links are keyboard accessible with clear focus indicators
- Color contrast meets WCAG 2.1 AA standards
- External links are properly identified for assistive technologies

### Responsive Behavior

- Icons maintain consistent sizing across all screen sizes
- Horizontal layout adapts to available space
- Touch targets meet minimum size requirements for mobile devices
- Icons wrap to multiple lines when necessary on smaller screens

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Include only the social media platforms where your organization actively maintains a presence
- Order platforms by importance or audience preference
- Keep the number of icons manageable (typically 3-6 platforms)
- Ensure all linked profiles are official and regularly updated
- Use the component in consistent locations across your site (e.g., footer, header)
- Verify that all URLs are correct and active

### Things to Avoid

- Don't include links to inactive or abandoned social media profiles
- Don't overwhelm users with too many social media options
- Avoid using custom or non-standard icons that users may not recognize
- Don't place social links in every section of every page
- Avoid linking to personal accounts instead of official organizational profiles
- Don't use social links as primary navigation elements

### When to Use Social Links

- Website footer for global social media presence
- Header or sidebar for persistent access
- Contact or "About Us" pages
- Event or program pages with dedicated social media channels
- Staff or department pages with specific social media accounts

## Testing

For comprehensive visual regression testing with all theme variations and configurations, see the [Visreg page](?path=/story/molecules-social-links-visreg--visreg).

## Related Components

- **Link**: Use for standalone links
- **Menu**: Use for primary navigation structures
- **Quick Links**: Consider when you need labeled text links instead of icons
